disease.name: Hypertension
disease.aliases: high blood pressure essential hypertension I10 htn bp
disease.overview: Hypertension is a chronic condition in which blood pressure remains persistently elevated (>=130/80 mmHg) and significantly increases the risk of cardiovascular disease, stroke, and kidney failure.
disease.symptoms: Often asymptomatic (known as the silent killer). Severe or uncontrolled cases may involve headache, dizziness, blurred vision, nosebleeds, or shortness of breath.
disease.management: Lifestyle modification (low-sodium diet, regular exercise, weight management, reduced alcohol), regular blood pressure monitoring, and antihypertensive medication such as ACE inhibitors, ARBs, beta-blockers, or calcium channel blockers when clinically indicated.

disease.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
disease.aliases: type 2 diabetes diabetes mellitus E11.9 high blood sugar hyperglycaemia t2dm
disease.overview: Type 2 diabetes is a chronic metabolic disorder characterised by insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency, resulting in persistently elevated blood glucose levels.
disease.symptoms: Increased thirst (polydipsia), frequent urination (polyuria), unexplained fatigue, blurred vision, slow-healing wounds, recurrent infections, and tingling or numbness in hands/feet.
disease.management: Lifestyle intervention (diet, exercise, weight loss), oral hypoglycaemics (Metformin first-line), SGLT2 inhibitors, GLP-1 agonists, insulin therapy when required. Regular HbA1c monitoring, lipid profile, renal function, and foot/eye examinations.
disease.source: WHO / MedlinePlus / ADA Standards of Care

disease.name: Upper Respiratory Infection
disease.aliases: URI cough mild fever J06.9 cold common cold pharyngitis rhinitis
disease.overview: Upper respiratory infection (URI) is a group of usually self-limiting infectious conditions affecting the nose, throat, sinuses, and upper airways. Most are caused by viruses.
disease.symptoms: Dry or productive cough, mild fever, sore throat, nasal congestion or rhinorrhoea, sneezing, fatigue, and mild body aches. Symptoms typically resolve in 7-10 days.
disease.management: Supportive care: adequate hydration, rest, analgesics/antipyretics for symptom relief. Antihistamines for rhinorrhoea. Antibiotics only if bacterial secondary infection is confirmed. Reassess if symptoms worsen beyond 10 days.

disease.name: Costochondritis
disease.aliases: chest wall pain costo M94.0 sternal pain musculoskeletal chest
disease.overview: Costochondritis is inflammation of the cartilage connecting the ribs to the sternum. It is a common cause of chest pain that is musculoskeletal in origin, not cardiac.
disease.symptoms: Sharp localised chest pain at the costo-sternal junction; pain worsens with deep breathing, coughing, or physical activity; reproducible tenderness on palpation of the affected joint.
disease.management: NSAIDs (ibuprofen 400-600mg TID with food) for 5-10 days, rest, avoidance of aggravating activities, gentle stretching. Seek urgent evaluation if cardiac cause cannot be excluded.

disease.name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
disease.aliases: PCOS polycystic ovarian syndrome E28.2 hormonal irregular period
disease.overview: PCOS is a common hormonal disorder affecting women of reproductive age, characterised by androgen excess, ovulatory dysfunction, and polycystic ovarian morphology.
disease.symptoms: Irregular or absent menstrual periods, excessive hair growth, acne, weight gain, thinning scalp hair, and difficulty conceiving. Associated with insulin resistance and metabolic syndrome.
disease.management: Lifestyle modification, combined oral contraceptives (COCs) to regulate cycles, metformin for insulin resistance, clomiphene or letrozole for ovulation induction.

disease.name: Migraine
disease.aliases: migraine headache G43 aura migraine with aura sumatriptan
disease.overview: Migraine is a neurological disorder characterised by recurrent moderate-to-severe headaches, often unilateral and pulsating, lasting 4-72 hours. May be preceded by aura.
disease.symptoms: Severe throbbing headache (often one-sided), nausea, vomiting, photophobia, phonophobia, and in some cases visual aura before the headache phase.
disease.management: Acute: triptans (sumatriptan), NSAIDs, anti-emetics. Preventive: beta-blockers, topiramate, amitriptyline. Magnesium and Vitamin B2 supplementation have evidence for prevention.
